name: Ovenbird
range: Breeds across eastern and northern North America; winters in Mexico, Central America, Caribbean, and northern South America
habitat: Mature deciduous and mixed forests with deep leaf litter
preferred food sources: insects, beetles, caterpillars, ants, spiders, snails, worms
nesting information: Ground nest shaped like a small domed oven, usually hidden in leaf litter
months you can see them: April, May, June, July, August, September, October
image credit: Rhododendrites, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Worm-eating Warbler
range: Breeds in eastern U.S.; winters in Mexico, Central America, and Caribbean
habitat: Steep wooded slopes, mature forest, dense understory
nesting information: Cup nest on or near the ground, often tucked into dry leaves on a slope
months you can see them: April, May, June, July, August, September
image credit: Bettina Arrigoni, CC BY 2.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Louisiana Waterthrush
range: Breeds in eastern U.S.; winters in Mexico, Central America, and Caribbean
habitat: Wooded streams, ravines, flowing water
preferred food sources: aquatic insects, insects, beetles, spiders, small crustaceans
nesting information: Nest placed in a bank, root mass, or cavity near running water
months you can see them: April, May, June, July, August, September
image credit: William H. Majoros, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Northern Waterthrush
range: Breeds across Canada and northern U.S.; winters from Mexico to northern South America
habitat: Boggy woods, swamps, wet thickets, stream edges
preferred food sources: aquatic insects, insects, spiders, snails, small crustaceans
nesting information: Cup nest on or near the ground in moss, roots, or dense wet vegetation
image credit: Rhododendrites, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Golden-winged Warbler
range: Breeds locally in Great Lakes and Appalachian regions; winters in Central and northern South America
habitat: Young forest, shrubby fields, regenerating woodland edges
nesting information: Ground nest hidden in grasses or low vegetation
image credit: Matt Felperin, CC BY 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Blue-winged Warbler
range: Breeds in eastern and central U.S.; winters in Central America and Caribbean
habitat: Shrubby fields, old fields, forest edges, young growth
nesting information: Ground nest hidden in grass, leaves, or low shrubs
months you can see them: April, May, June, July, August, September
image credit: Bettina Arrigoni, CC BY 2.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Black-and-white Warbler
range: Breeds across eastern North America; winters in southeastern U.S., Mexico, Caribbean, and Central America
habitat: Deciduous and mixed forest, especially trunks and large branches
nesting information: Cup nest on the ground, often tucked against a log, rock, or tree base
months you can see them: April, May, June, July, August, September, October
image credit: Rhododendrites, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Prothonotary Warbler
range: Breeds in southeastern and midwestern U.S.; winters in Central America and northern South America
habitat: Wooded swamps, flooded forests, bottomlands
preferred food sources: insects, aquatic insects, beetles, spiders, snails
nesting information: Cavity nest in tree holes, stumps, or nest boxes near water
months you can see them: April, May, June, July, August, September
image credit: Rhododendrites, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Tennessee Warbler
range: Breeds in Canadian boreal forest; migrates through much of eastern North America; winters in Central and northern South America
habitat: Boreal forest in summer; mixed woodland and edges during migration
nesting information: Ground nest hidden in moss or low vegetation
image credit: Cephas, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Orange-crowned Warbler
range: Breeds in Canada, Alaska, and western mountains; winters in southern U.S. and Mexico
habitat: Shrubby woodland, brushy edges, riparian thickets
nesting information: Cup nest on or near the ground in dense vegetation
image credit: Vickie J Anderson, http://www.wildlifeimagesupclose.com, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Nashville Warbler
range: Breeds in northern and western North America; winters in Mexico and Central America
habitat: Brushy openings, young forests, woodland edges
nesting information: Ground nest hidden in moss, grass, or low vegetation
image credit: Bmajoros, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Connecticut Warbler
range: Breeds in boreal bogs and northern forest; winters in South America
habitat: Boggy boreal forest, tamarack, spruce, dense understory
nesting information: Ground nest hidden in moss or dense low vegetation
image credit: ALAN SCHMIERER from southeast AZ, USA, CC0, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Mourning Warbler
range: Breeds in northeastern and north-central North America; winters in Central and South America
habitat: Young forest, dense thickets, regenerating clearings
nesting information: Ground or low cup nest hidden in thick vegetation
image credit: kirk gardner, CC BY 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Kentucky Warbler
range: Breeds in eastern U.S.; winters in Mexico, Central America, and northern South America
habitat: Rich deciduous forest with dense understory
nesting information: Ground nest hidden in leaves at base of shrubs or saplings
months you can see them: April, May, June, July, August, September
image credit: Adam Jackson, CC0, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Common Yellowthroat
range: Breeds across much of North America; winters in southern U.S., Mexico, Caribbean, and Central America
habitat: Marshes, wet fields, brushy edges, dense low vegetation
nesting information: Cup nest low in grasses, reeds, or shrubs
months you can see them: April, May, June, July, August, September, October
image credit: Rhododendrites, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Hooded Warbler
range: Breeds in eastern U.S.; winters in Mexico, Caribbean, and Central America
habitat: Mature forest with dense understory, ravines, wooded wetlands
nesting information: Cup nest low in shrubs or saplings
image credit: Ninahale, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: American Redstart
range: Breeds across much of North America; winters in Mexico, Caribbean, Central America, and northern South America
habitat: Deciduous forest, second growth, wooded edges, shade trees
nesting information: Cup nest in fork of shrub or small tree
image credit: Wildreturn, CC BY 2.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Cape May Warbler
range: Breeds in Canadian boreal spruce-fir forest; winters in Caribbean
habitat: Spruce-fir forest in summer; flowering trees and edges in winter
nesting information: Cup nest high in conifers, often spruce
image credit: Derek Spencer, CC BY 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Cerulean Warbler
range: Breeds in eastern deciduous forests; winters in northern South America
habitat: Mature deciduous forest, forested ridges, tall canopy
nesting information: Cup nest high on horizontal tree branches
image credit: U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service Headquarters, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Northern Parula
range: Breeds in eastern North America; winters in Florida, Caribbean, Mexico, and Central America
habitat: Mature forest with epiphytes, moss, lichens, or Spanish moss
nesting information: Nest in hanging moss, lichens, or epiphyte clumps
image credit: Dan Pancamo, CC BY-SA 2.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Magnolia Warbler
range: Breeds in Canadian boreal and northern forests; winters in Mexico, Central America, and Caribbean
habitat: Young coniferous and mixed forest, especially spruce-fir
nesting information: Cup nest low in dense conifers
image credit: Rhododendrites, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Bay-breasted Warbler
range: Breeds in Canadian boreal forest; winters in Central and northern South America
habitat: Spruce-fir forest, especially during spruce budworm outbreaks
nesting information: Cup nest in conifers, often spruce or fir
image credit: Mdf, CC BY-SA 3.0 <http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Blackburnian Warbler
range: Breeds in northeastern and Appalachian forests; winters in northern South America
habitat: Mature coniferous and mixed forest, high canopy
nesting information: Cup nest high in conifer branches
image credit: Rhododendrites, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Northern Yellow Warbler
range: Breeds across much of North America; winters in Mexico, Central America, and northern South America
habitat: Willows, wet thickets, stream edges, shrubby wetlands
nesting information: Cup nest in shrubs or small trees, often willows
months you can see them: April, May, June, July, August, September
image credit: Paul Danese, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Chestnut-sided Warbler
range: Breeds in northeastern and north-central North America; winters in Central America
habitat: Young forest, brushy clearings, regenerating woodland
nesting information: Cup nest low in shrubs, briars, or saplings
image credit: Mdf, CC BY-SA 3.0 <http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Blackpoll Warbler
range: Breeds across boreal Canada and Alaska; winters in northern South America
habitat: Boreal spruce-fir forest, tundra-edge shrubs
nesting information: Cup nest low in spruce or shrubs
image credit: Wildreturn, CC BY 2.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Black-throated Blue Warbler
range: Breeds in northeastern and Appalachian forests; winters in Caribbean
habitat: Mature deciduous and mixed forest with dense understory
nesting information: Cup nest in low shrubs, saplings, or dense understory
image credit: Rhododendrites, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Palm Warbler
range: Breeds in bogs and boreal wetlands; winters in southeastern U.S., Caribbean, and Central America
habitat: Boreal bogs, open wetlands, weedy fields during migration
preferred food sources: insects, beetles, caterpillars, spiders, seeds, berries
nesting information: Ground nest in bog vegetation, often near sphagnum moss
image credit: Rhododendrites, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Pine Warbler
range: Breeds in eastern pine forests; winters in southeastern U.S. and Caribbean
habitat: Pine woods, pine plantations, mixed pine-oak forest
nesting information: Cup nest high in pine trees
months you can see them: March, April, May, September, October, November
image credit: Vkulikov, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Yellow-rumped Warbler
range: Breeds across northern and western North America; winters widely in U.S., Mexico, and Central America
habitat: Coniferous and mixed forests; open woods and coastal scrub in winter
nesting information: Cup nest in conifers, often on horizontal branches
months you can see them: March, April, May, September, October, November
image credit: Rhododendrites, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Yellow-throated Warbler
range: Breeds in southeastern and central U.S.; winters in Florida, Caribbean, Mexico, and Central America
habitat: Sycamore-lined rivers, cypress swamps, pine woods
nesting information: Cup nest high in trees, often sycamore, pine, or cypress
months you can see them: April, May, June, July, August, September
image credit: Wildreturn, CC BY 2.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Prairie Warbler
range: Breeds in eastern U.S.; winters in Florida, Caribbean, and Central America
habitat: Shrubby fields, pine barrens, young forest, dry scrub
nesting information: Cup nest low in shrubs or saplings
image credit: Wildreturn, CC BY 2.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Black-throated Green Warbler
range: Breeds in eastern and northern forests; winters in Mexico, Central America, and Caribbean
habitat: Coniferous and mixed forests, hemlock, spruce, pine
nesting information: Cup nest in conifers or mixed forest trees
image credit: Rhododendrites, CC BY-SA 4.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0>, via Wikimedia Commons

name: Canada Warbler
range: Breeds in Canada and northeastern U.S.; winters in northern South America
habitat: Moist forest, streamside thickets, boggy woods, dense understory
nesting information: Ground or low nest hidden in moss, roots, or dense vegetation
image credit: William H. Majoros, CC BY-SA 3.0 <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0>, via Wikimedia Commons
